samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts. First is dual-port RAM. The FIFO data is stored in the RAM. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule. The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata.

Second part is data write controller. It contains write pointer, write full, write address and write increment. The write pointer is incremented by write increment and synchronized by read clock. The write full is determined by comparing the Gray code of the write pointer and the synchronized read pointer. The write address is the lower bits of the write pointer. The write increment is triggered by the write enable signal.

Third part is data read controller. It contains read pointer, read empty, read address and read increment. The read pointer is incremented by read increment and synchronized by write clock. The read empty is determined by comparing the Gray code of the synchronized write pointer and the read pointer. The read address is the lower bits of the read pointer. The read increment is triggered by the read enable signal.

Fourth part is read pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of write clock. It is used to collect the write pointer and output to the data write controller. The purpose is to ensure the write pointer is stable and not change during the read operation.

Fifth part is write pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of read clock. It is used to collect the read pointer and output to the data read controller. The purpose is to ensure the read pointer is stable and not change during the write operation.

As for the method of empty and full judgment, the FIFO uses the method of comparing the Gray code of the read pointer and the write pointer. When the Gray code of the read pointer is equal to the inverted MSB of the write pointer and the remaining bits are the same, the FIFO is full. When the Gray code of the write pointer is equal to the read pointer, the FIFO is empty.

For the implementation, the dual-port RAM is instantiated as a submodule. The ports are connected by the corresponding input and output signals. The write enable signal is winc, the write address is waddr, the write data is wdata, the read enable signal is rinc, the read address is raddr, the read data is rdata.

For the write pointer, the write pointer is incremented by the write increment signal. When the write pointer equals to the inverted MSB of the read pointer and the remaining bits are the same, the wfull is set to 1. For the read pointer, the read pointer is incremented by the read increment signal. When the read pointer equals to the write pointer, the rempty is set to 1.
